translated: And if he does not forgive others their mistakes, his Father who is in heaven will also not forgive his mistakes.

ref: So they sealed it tight and placed soldiers there to guard it.
text: Na bɛkataa boɔ he ano, de nsɔano hyɛɛ no nso maa awɛmfoɔ he wɛne Yesu damena he.
translated: And they covered the entrance of the tomb, and sealed it, and set guards to watch over Jesus' tomb.

ref: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
text: Afei Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Honnsuro; honkɔ nkɔbɔ me nuanom amaneɛ sɛ bɛnkɔ Galilea; na bebehu me hɔ.” Awɛmfoɔ he amanebɔ
translated: Then Jesus said to them, "Do not be afraid; go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee; and there they will see me." The guards reported.

ref: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
text: Na ne ho hu nti, awɛmfoɔ he ho popoeɛ, na bɛyɛɛ sɛ nea bɛawuwu.
translated: And because he was afraid, the guards trembled and became as if they were dead.

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: Saa nna he mu he, Yesu firi Nasaret a ɔya Galilea baeɛ, na Yohane bɔɔ no asu Yordan mu.
translated: In those days, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan.

ref: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
text: Na Yesu nyaa tema maa no, na ɔtenee ne nsa kaa no, na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Mepɛ, wo ho mfiri.”
translated: And Jesus had compassion on him, and he stretched out his hand and touched him, and he said to him, “I am willing, be clean.

ref: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
text: Na befirii hyiadane he muaa pɛ, bɛ ne Yakobo ne Yohane kɔɔ Simon ne Andreo fie.
translated: And immediately after the meeting, they, with James and John, went to Simon and Andrew's house.

ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Na Simon asebaa yare abunu da hɔ. Na ntɛmaa bɛkaa ne ho asɛm kyerɛɛ Yesu.
translated: And Simon's mother-in-law was sick with a fever. And immediately they told Jesus about her.

ref: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
text: Na kuro mũ he nyinaa behyiaa pono he ano.
translated: And everyone in the town met at the doorway.

ref: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
text: Anɔpa a animu nteteeɛ he, Yesu sɔre kɔhyɛɛ kokoam baabi, na ɔbɔɔ mpaeɛ hɔ.
translated: Very early in the morning, Jesus got up, went to a solitary place, and prayed there.

ref: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
text: “Ɛɛne nti ne ɔkasa saa? Ɔyɛ abususɛm! Hwane ne tumi de bɔne kyɛ ka Nyankopɔn ho?”
translated: Why does he speak like that? It is blasphemy! Who has the power to forgive sins but God?

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
text: Yesu bisaa bɛ sɛ, “Nnipa a bɛkɔ ayefohyia ntumi nkyene kɔm berɛ a ayefokunu he ya bɛ nkyɛne, ɔnte saa’? Mmerɛ tentene a ayefokunu he ne bɛya hɔ he, ɔnsɛ sɛ bɛkyene kɔm.
translated: Jesus asked them, "Can people who go to a wedding fast while the bridegroom is with them, isn't that so?" As long as the bridegroom is with them, they should not fast.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
text: Yei maa Farisifoɔ he bisaa no sɛ, “Hwɛ, mmɛ nti ne bɛɛyɔ deɛ otia mmara homeda?”
translated: Then the Pharisees asked him, "Look, why do your disciples do what is unlawful on the Sabbath?

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Yesu kɔɔ bepɔ he so, na ɔtoo ne nsa frɛɛ bɛ a ohia bɛ ma bɛbaa ne nkyɛne.
translated: Jesus went up a mountain, and he invited those who needed him to come to him.

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Na atwerɛfoɔ he a befiri Yerusalem baeɛ he kaa sɛ, “Ɔwɔ Beelsebul, na ɔde ahonhommɔne panyini he tumidie ne tutu ahonhom fi.”
translated: And the scribes who came from Jerusalem were saying, "He has Beelzebul, and by the power of the chief of demons he casts out demons.

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: Nɔkorɛ, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, ɔde nipa bɔne ne abususɛm biaa a ɔbɛka bɛkyɛ no,
translated: Truly, I have told him that he will use bad people and any insults he says will be spread by him.

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Nea ɔyɔ Nyankopɔn apɛdeɛ biaa yɛ me nua beema ne me nua baa ne me maame.”
translated: Whoever does the will of God is my brother, my sister, and my mother.

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Na afei nso, Yesu hyɛɛ aseɛ maa nkyerɛkyerɛ po he ho. Nnipadɔm betwaa ne ho hyiaeɛ ma ɔkɔforoo kodoɔ bi po he so na ɔtenaa mu, berɛ a na nnipadɔm he tete po he ano.
translated: And then also, Jesus began to teach there. The crowd gathered around him, so he went up into a boat on the sea and sat in it, while the crowd stood on the shore.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Na aba he bi koguu nkasɛem, na nkasɛe he nyiniiɛ he, omiaa no, na anso aba biaa.
translated: When a mango falls to the ground, and takes root there, it grows, and it also bears mangoes.

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
text: Nso ɔnam sɛ benni nhini nti, begyina kakra bi, na sɛ haw anaa taa ba bɛ so ɔnam asɛm he nti a, bɛhwe ase ntɛm so.
translated: But because they have no roots, they will stand for a little while, and if trouble or persecution comes upon them because of the word, they will fall quickly.

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: Ɔte sɛ sinapi aba a sɛ bedua ne asaase mu a, ɔno ne aba a osua paa asaase so aba nyinaa mu;
translated: It is like a mustard seed which, when sown in the ground, is the smallest of all the seeds on the earth;

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
text: Nso na Yesu ada summiiɛ so ya kodoɔ he mu; na benyanee no bisaa no sɛ, “Kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, yɛɛyera yi, ɔmfa wo ho?”
translated: But then Jesus had gone to a quiet place to pray; and his disciples asked him, saying, “Teacher, we are perishing, don't you care?

ref: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
text: Onyaneeɛ, na ɔkaa mframa he anim ka kyerɛɛ po he sɛ, “Gyae, yɛ dinn!” Na mframa he ano brɛɛ ase, na ɔyɛɛ dinn.
translated: He awoke, and he rebuked the wind and said to the sea, "Silence! Be still!" And the wind ceased, and it became still.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
text: Obisaa bɛ sɛ, “Ɛɛne nti ne hosuro? Nti obesi nnɛ yi honni gyidie?”
translated: He asked him, "Why the fear? So will he build today's faith?

ref: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: Na owuraa fie hɔ he, obisaa bɛ sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne hooyɔ gyegyeegye na hoosu yi? Akwadaa he nwuiɛ, na waada.”
translated: And he entered the house, he asked saying, "Why all this noise and crying? The child is not dead, he is sleeping.

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Na bɛbaa Yesu hɔ, na bebehuu nea kane he na ɔwɔ ahonhommɔne a na bɛfrɛ no Dɔm he sɛ ɔte hɔ fira ntoma na n’adwene mu ate. Na besuroeɛ.
translated: And they came to Jesus, and saw the one who had previously had evil spirits, whom they called Legion, sitting there clothed and in his right mind. And they were afraid.

ref: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
text: Na bɛseree no. Na ɔmaa bɛ nyinaa kɔɔ abɔntene, na ɔfaa akwadaa he papa ne ne maame ne bɛ a bɛka ne ho he, na ɔkɔɔ baabi a akwadaa he ya he.
translated: And they pleaded with him. Then he sent everyone outside, and he took the child's father and mother and those who were with him, and he went to where the child was.

ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: Na afei berɛ a Yesu tware po he bio sane ba n’agya he, nnipadɔm kɛseɛ twaa ne ho hyiaeɛ, na ɔya po he ho.
translated: And then when Jesus crossed the sea again and returned to his father, a great crowd surrounded him, and he healed them.

ref: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
text: Na waate Yesu ho asɛm, na ofirii n’akyi nnipadɔm he mu bɛkaa n’ataadeɛ ano,
translated: And she had heard about Jesus, and she came from behind him in the crowd and touched the edge of his garment.

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Na ɔhyɛɛ bɛ ketee sɛ bɛmma biaa nte yei, na afei ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, bɛmma akwadaa he bibi nni.
translated: And he strictly commanded them that no one should hear this, and then he told them that they should not give the child anything.

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Baabi a hobɛkɔ a bɛannye ho he, na bɛantie ho he, sɛ hoofiri hɔ a, homporoporo ho nane ase mfuturo ngu hɔ, na ɔnyɛ adanseɛ ntia bɛ.”
translated: When you go to a place where they do not receive you or listen to you, when you leave there, shake off the dust from your feet onto the ground, so that it may not be a testimony against them.

ref: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it

ref: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.

translated: Because Herod feared John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, he protected him. Whenever he listened to him, he was greatly perplexed, yet he liked to listen to him.

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”

text: Na Yohane asuafoɔ he tee asɛm he, bɛba bɛfaa n’amu he na bɛkɔpɛɛ no koraberɛ bodane bi mu. Yesu maa Nnipa Mpem Nnum Aduane
translated: And John's disciples heard about it, they came and took his body and laid him in a tomb. Jesus fed Five Thousand People.
